As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JEANNIE TSAI VS. LEA JOURNO COSMETIQUE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 11, 2021, Line 5. DEFENDANT DAWN SERPA DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT (Part 1 of 2) Serpa's demurrer to amended complaint is sustained and overruled as follows: The demurrer to cause of action 3 for "Fraud in the Execution or Inception" is sustained without leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e).) The allegations of the amended complaint show that plaintiff knew she was entering into a loan with the defendants and was fraudulently induced to do so based on defendants' alleged misrepresentations. (See, e.g., Amended Complaint, pars. 3-4.) Plaintiff pleads a classic fraud in the inducement claim and there are no facts to support a fraud in the execution claim. In Rosenthal v. Great Western Fin. Securities Corp. (1996) 14 Cal.4th 394, 415, the court explains that fraud in the execution exists where the party "does not know what he is signing" while "[f]raud in the inducement [] occurs when the promisor knows what he is signing but his consent is induced by fraud." (emphasis in original.) The demurrer to cause of action 4 for "Fraud in the Factum" is sustained without leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e).) The title of this claim is also a fraud in the execution claim, but plaintiff has clarified that it is really a CC sec. 1572 "catch all" fraud claim. (See Fruestta v. Hauben (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 551, 556 ["Professor Williston defines fraud in the factum as 'such fraud in the procurement of the execution of a note or bill as results in the signer's being ignorant of the nature of the instrument he is signing'."].) Notwithstanding plaintiff's clarification, the Court dismisses the claim as duplicative. There is no salient difference between causes of action 4 and 2 and plaintiff can vindicate her rights by prosecuting cause of action 2. The demurrer to cause of action 9 for "Securities Violations" is sustained without leave to amend. (See CCP sec. 430.10(e).) Plaintiff concedes that the claim/s are time barred. The demurrer to cause of action 10 for "Breach of Promissory Note" is overruled. Per CCP sec. 452, the Court liberally reads the amended complaint and plaintiff pleads the existence of a written contract. A plain reading of the complaint indicates that the parties entered into a written agreement memorialized by a "note." A "promissory note" has long been held to be a "contract in writing and in the event of an ambiguity appearing on its face is to be construed as any other written contract." (Trigg v. Arnott (1937) 22 Cal.App.2d 455, 457; see also Saks v. Charity Mission Baptist Church (2001) 90 Cal.App.4th 1116, 1132 [promissory note is a signed negotiable instrument].) Serpa's reply argues that the cause of action does not sufficiently allege the terms of the contract or attach a copy. That is a new argument based on CCP 430.10(e) and the demurrer was based on CCP 430.10(g). The new argument is rejected based on due process grounds. (end of part 1 of 2) =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JEANNIE TSAI VS. LEA JOURNO COSMETIQUE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 11, 2021, Line 5. DEFENDANT DAWN SERPA'S Motion To Strike Amended Complaint Serpa's motion to strike is denied as to the purported "puffery" or "opinion" allegations. The allegations are not false, improper, or irrelevant per CCP sec. 436(a). A factual issue remains whether Serpa possessed "superior knowledge" such that these allegations can support plaintiff's fraud claims. (See Brakke v. Economic Concepts, Inc. (2013) 213 Cal.App.4th 761, 769 [explaining when opinion can support fraud claim]; Jolley v. Chase Home Finance, LLC (2013) 213 Cal.App.4th 872, 892 [same].) The motion to strike is granted without leave to amend as to paragraphs 135, 136, and 137. These paragraphs allege violations of various securities laws to support the ninth cause of action. All of the claims are time barred, however, and they are therefore irrelevant. The simple allegations at paragraphs 135-137 that Serpa violated securities laws do not support the fraud claims. Indeed, those allegations are not incorporated into the fraud claims. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for Serpa is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

DEFENDANT LEA JOURNO COSMETIQUE, LLC'S Joinder In Defendant Dawn Serpa'S Motion To Strike Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint Lea's joinder to Serpa's demurrer and motion to strike amended complaint is off calendar. Plaintiff demonstrates that Lea is a suspended entity and therefore, it cannot prosecute or defend an action until it obtains a certificate of revivor. (See Plaintiff's RJN, Ex. A; Kaufman & Broad Communities, Inc. v. Performance Plastering (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 212, 217-218.) Lea's reply states that it paid the outstanding taxes and submitted an application for revivor. Lea has yet to receive the certificate of revivor, however. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. rulings.Case Title: JEANNIE TSAI VS. LEA JOURNO COSMETIQUE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 11, 2021, Line 5. DEFENDANTS JEROME A. BELLOTTI & ASSOCIATES, JEROME BELLOTTI'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Belotti's joinder to Serpa's demurrer to amended complaint is off calendar. Belotti answered the amended complaint on November 6, 2020, and filed its joinder to the demurrer on April 1, 2021. Belotti waived the right to demurrer because it was obligated to do so "at the same time" it answered. (CCP secs. 430.30(c); 472a(a).) rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion Opposing Co-Defendant Chi Kong Cheung'S Application For Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 11, 2021, Line 6. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion Opposing Co-Defendant Chi Kong Cheung'S Application For Good Faith Settlement Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion opposing co-defendant Chi Kong Cheung's application for good faith settlement determination is denied and Cheung's application is granted. Cheung's $250,000 settlement negotiated with plaintiffs is for his auto insurance policy's limits. Considering the factors set out in Tech-Bilt v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499, the settlement is in good faith. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL J. MOATS ET AL VS. MICHAEL TSANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 11, 2021, Line 2, Plaintiffs' Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Peter Vestal, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: JPT Vestal discloses that he was a client of the moving parties' law firm more than ten years ago. The motion of Michael J. Moats and Arlene Pons ("Plaintiffs") is GRANTED as follows: Defendant Addy Wong d/b/a W Construction Co. ("Defendant") shall serve verified code-compliant responses, without objections, to Plaintiffs' Form Interrogatories - Construction Litigation (Set One), Special Interrogatories (Set One) and Amended Request for Production of Documents (Set One) within 15 days of the court's order. The term "code-compliant" means in compliance with the California Civil Discovery Act, Code of Civil Procedure sections 2016.010 et seq., as interpreted by the decisions of the courts of this state. Plaintiffs' request for monetary sanctions against Defendant and his counsel of record is GRANTED in the amount of $2,090. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pvestal@nvlawllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 11, 2021, Line 3, Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents And Form Interrogs. Pro Tem Judge Peter Vestal, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. Regents") shall serve a verified code-compliant response, without objections, to Ms. Schibler's Form Interrogatories (Set One) No. 15.1 within 15 days of the court's order. U.C. Regents shall also serve code-compliant responses to Form Interrogatories (Set One) Nos. 16.1 and 16.2, as well as to Request for Production of Documents (Set One) Nos. 1 - 7 within 15 days of the court's order. The term "code-compliant" means in compliance with the California Civil Discovery Act, Code of Civil Procedure sections 2016.010 et seq., as interpreted by the decisions of the courts of this state, including, without limitation, the case decisions cited in Ms. Schibler's moving papers. Ms. Schibler's request for monetary sanctions against U.C. Regents is GRANTED in the amount of $2,800. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pvestal@nvlawllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BAYCREST TOWERS RESIDENTIAL CONDOMINIUM VS. ANITA CHUGANI ET AL

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 11, 2021, Line 4, PLAINTIFF BAYCREST TOWERS RESIDENTIAL CONDOMINIUM's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Production Of Documents (Part 1 of 2 for purposes of entry of Tentative Ruling). If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 11, 2021, Line 4, PLAINTIFF BAYCREST TOWERS RESIDENTIAL CONDOMINIUM's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Production Of Documents (Part 2 of 2 for purposes of entry of Tentative Ruling). As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pvestal@nvlawllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. (End of Tentative Ruling) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THAU BICH LY VS. MATTHEW MCDONALD OLIVERI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 11, 2021, Line 9. DEFENDANT OLIVERI LAW, LLP, MATTHEW OLIVERI DEMURRER to COMPLAINT Defendants Oliveri Law, LLP and Matthew Oliveri's demurrer is ordered off calendar for failure to comply with CCP 430.41 and because the former is in default. Default was entered against Oliveri Law, LLP on January 15, 2021, which foreclosures any demurrer by it. (Devlin v. Kearny Mesa AMC/Jeep/Renault, Inc. (1984) 155 Cal.App.3d 381, 385.) However, counsel's declaration does not say that issue was addressed in a meet and confer. (See CCP 430.41(a).) Moreover, defendants' reply appears to argue that the default was improperly entered, but the register of actions reveals no motion to that effect. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ROY MILLER ET AL VS. MACS DEVELOPMENT LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 11, 2021, Line 11. PLAINTIFFS ROY MILLER, MARIO GALANDE'S Motion To Stay Action Grant pursuant to defendant's notice of non-opposition filed on April 27, 2021. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing.=(302/RBU)
